title: Slack OAuth2 missing required scopes
symptom: When attempting to invite users to a private channel, Slack returns a 'missing_scope' error indicating that required scopes are not provided.
applies_when: Using Slack node to invite users
OAuth2 credentials are not configured with required scopes
not_this_if: Using a bot token with sufficient scopes
Scopes are correctly set in the OAuth2 configuration
root_cause: The Slack OAuth2 credentials are hard-coded with a limited set of scopes, which do not include the necessary permissions for inviting users to private channels. This results in the 'missing_scope' error when the API is called.
fix_steps: Create a new application at api.slack.com and configure it with the required User Token Scopes: 'groups:write' and 'groups:write.invites'.
Install the app to your workspace and obtain the User OAuth Token.
In the n8n Slack node, create a new credential using the User OAuth Token.
Test the connection to ensure it works and then proceed with inviting users to the channel.
agent_instruction: Create a new Slack app, configure the necessary scopes, and use the User OAuth Token in n8n.
